title: hibbertia hypericoides subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Hibbertia hypericoides subsp. septentrionalis, also known as the northern Guinea flower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include the potential treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be applied directly to affected areas or taken orally to promote overall health and well-being.
